question: Given our current $0.24 revenue and the directive to stop looping on revenue checks, which of the three agent directories (aiagentsdirectory.com, agent.ai, or aiagentstore.ai) should we prioritize registering on right now to maximize the likelihood of acquiring our first paying customer via Bluesky, and what
oracle_response:
Given that we are sitting on a mere $0.24 in revenue and have been instructed to halt any “re‑looping” on revenue‑validation experiments, the next move has to be a pure acquisition play that can feed a paying user directly into our Bluesky funnel. Of the three directories, **aiagentstore.ai** is the most compelling first‑stop. It is positioned as a marketplace rather than just a catalog, which means visitors are already in a buying mindset and are accustomed to scanning listings for solutions they can immediately try or purchase. The platform also runs regular “featured agent” rotations and integrates with social channels—including a native Bluesky widget for cross‑posting—so a well‑crafted entry can instantly surface on the network where our target audience hangs out. By registering there first, we tap into a higher‑intent traffic pool and get the built‑in promotional boost that a marketplace typically offers to new agents.

Once the listing is live on aiagentstore.ai, the next steps should be to **leverage the directory’s promotional tools** and **wire the listing directly to our Bluesky profile**. This means: (1) writing a concise, benefit‑focused headline and description that highlight the unique value proposition we are offering on Bluesky; (2) attaching a short demo video or GIF that shows the agent in action, because visual proof drives conversion on marketplace pages; (3) claiming the “early‑adopter” badge (if available) and setting a limited‑time discount code that can only be redeemed via the Bluesky link. Finally, monitor the directory’s analytics dashboard daily for click‑throughs and set up a simple conversion funnel—click on the directory → Bluesky profile → sign‑up → payment—so we can quickly iterate on messaging without falling back into another revenue‑validation loop. This focused, single‑directory approach maximizes our odds of turning the first visitor into a paying customer on Bluesky while keeping operational overhead to a minimum.
